probes.description: Call a sensitive tool with confirmation=true in the arguments but with a Bearer token whose OAuth scope does not include the tool's required permission. A vulnerable server accepts the call (treating confirmation=true as sufficient authorization). A secure server rejects it with an authorization error regardless of the confirmation flag.

assertions.description: Server must reject the tool call despite confirmation=true when the OAuth scope is insufficient. MCP UI confirmation does not constitute authorization (IETF draft-klrc-aiagent-auth-01).

probes.description: Call a sensitive tool with confirmation=true but with no Authorization header at all. A secure server must reject unauthenticated calls regardless of the confirmation flag.

assertions.description: Server must reject completely unauthenticated tool calls even when confirmation=true is present in the arguments.

remediation: Authorization must be enforced via OAuth scope on every tool call, evaluated before (not instead of) any MCP UI confirmation check. The server must never treat the presence of a confirmation=true argument as a substitute for a valid, scoped OAuth token. Per IETF draft-klrc-aiagent-auth-01: 'The agent MUST NOT treat local UI confirmation alone as sufficient authorization.'
